title: GSR 616(E) 20.09.2006: Exemption of recombinant pharmaceutical falling in specific category for Rules 7 to 10 under The Manufacture, Use, Import, Export and Storage of Hazardous Micro Organisms/ Genetically Engineered Organisms or Cells Rules, 1989

: MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT AND FORESTS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 20th September, 2006 G.SR.616(E)— Whereas the Ministry of Environment and Forests has notified the rules for the Manufacture, Use, Import, Export and Storage of Hazardous Microorganisms / Genetically Engineered Organisms or Cells, Rules 1989 hereinafter referred to as Rules vide No, GSR 1037 (E) dated 5" December 1989, -- And whereas rule 20 of the rules for the Manufacture, Use, Import, Export and Storage of Hazardous Microorganisms / Genetically Engineered Organisms or Cels, Rules 1989 empowers the Ministry of Environment and Forests to exempt an occupler handiing a particular microorganism/genetically engineered organism from the application of the provisions of Rules 7 to11 (both inclusive). Now, therefore, in pursuance of rule 20 of the rules for the Manufecture, Use, Tmport, Export and Storage of Hazardous Microorganisms / Genetically Engineered Organisms or Cells, Rules 1989, the Central Government hereby considers it necessary that the manufacturer and/or importer of recombinant pharmaceuticals falling in the following categories may be exempted from the provisions of rules 7 to 10 (both inclusive) of the above said Rules with effect from the date of publication of this notification i the official gazette. (a) Indigenous product development, manufacture and marketing of pharmaceutical products derived from organisms falling under Risk Group I and Group 11 as specified in the Recombinant DNA Safety Guidelines, 1990, (b} Import and marketing of products derived from Living Modified Organisrns (L¥0s) as Drugs and Pharmaceuticals in bulk and/or finished formulations where the end product being imported is not a Living Modified Organisms, [F. No. 12/7/2004-CS] DESHDEEPAK VERMA, Jt.
